recommendations.action: Continue current renal diet; consider protein reduction to ~0.4 g/kg DM if creatinine rises or proteinuria worsens.
Add phosphate binder (e.g., sevelamer 10 mg/kg PO q12h) to target serum phosphorus <5 mg/dL and reduce calcium‑phosphate deposition.
Monitor systolic blood pressure; if >140 mmHg, titrate ACE‑inhibitor dose or add low-dose diuretic (furosemide 0.1–0.2 mg/kg PO q12h).
Recheck CBC, serum biochemistry, urinalysis, urine protein/creatinine ratio, and electrolytes in 6 weeks; adjust therapy based on trends.
Educate owner to monitor activity level, water intake, appetite, and signs of azotemia (vomiting, lethargy); encourage regular hydration and avoid nephrotoxic drugs.
Schedule follow‑up visit in 8 weeks or sooner if clinical status changes.
